description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - Duplicate Address Detection (DAD) status indicated a duplicate for IPv6 anycast gateway configuration. As a workaround, configure an active gateway IPv6 address and SVI IPv6 address, as this can prevent DAD issues. Issuing the commands interface shut followed by interface no-shut can help in resolving the issue.

description: After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what limitation is mentioned for ARP?

Feature Caveat: ARP - After an issue with duplicate IPs on two VXLAN tunnel endpoints (VTEPs) is resolved, the kernel will have the ARP entry, but the output of the show arp and show evpn mac-ip commands do not show that IP. As a workaround, flap the physical interface.

description: The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for BGP?

Feature Caveat: BGP - The next-hop-unchanged option needs to be explicitly configured to preserve nexthop while advertising routes to eBGP peers, in the L2VPN EVPN address-family. For example: Copy router bgp.

description: Symptom: When wireless clients roam between access points connected to the same switch, they lose connectivity and receive an APIPA address. Connectivity is only restored after the MAC-age timer expires. Scenario: When a client is authenticated with a VLAN on a secure port, the VLAN is added as an ingress-only VLAN on other secure ports, but the MSTP state for those ports remains in blocked state instead of forwarding state, preventing seamless client movement. Workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in "device-mode" instead of "client-mode":{color:#000000}interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device{color}

workaround: Reduce the MAC-age timer to minimize the impact. or configure AP ports in "device-mode" instead of "client-mode":{color:#000000}interface 1/1/X port-access client-limit 1 port-access client-mode device{color}

description: Symptom: Phyport is missing on a remote VTEP ARP table when a shut/no shut is performed on interfaces of the local host connected in local VTEP VSX pairs. Scenario: This issue can occur when EVPN sends the MAC modify events with a different order to NDMD (like update/delete, or delete/add mac) as the existing NDMD design does not handle such scenarios. Workaround: Clear ARP on the local VTEP.
symptom: Phyport is missing on a remote VTEP ARP table when a shut/no shut is performed on interfaces of the local host connected in local VTEP VSX pairs.
scenario: s.
workaround: Clear ARP on the local VTEP.

description: Symptom: When a fan tray is installed into a running JL635A, the newly installed fan tray may be unable to start spinning and the fans on the fan tray may show a status of faulted. Scenario: This issue can occur when a fan tray is installed into a running JL635A. Workaround: A cold reboot of the JL635A with all 6 fan trays installed allows all 6 fan trays to start spinning and running normally.

description: Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.
symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured.
scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log.
workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

description: Symptom: HPE-routing crash. Scenario: Upgrading to 10.15.1010 leads to a crash.
symptom: HPE-routing crash.
scenario: Upgrading to 10.15.1010 leads to a crash.

messages.content: For 8325 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what is the workaround for EVPN Bug 370458?
EVPN (Bug ID 370458): No workaround is documented in the release notes.

description: Symptom: The switch may reboot with the following failure messages: Software thermal reset Insufficient fans Fan tray -1/4/1 faulted with the reason "common error". Scenario: This issue occurs due to resource contention or high software stress. Workaround: Update to a different code version.
